en.extract:
The olive long-tailed cuckoo (Cercococcyx olivinus) is a species of cuckoo in the family Cuculidae.
It is found throughout the African tropical rainforest.
It lays its nests on the ground, and forages from the canopy of trees.
Olive long-tailed cuckoos do not flock in groups.

The '''olive long-tailed cuckoo''' ('''''Cercococcyx olivinus''''') is a species of [[cuckoo]] in the family [[Cuculidae]].
It is found throughout the [[Tropical forest|African tropical rainforest]].

It lays its nests on the ground,<ref>{{Cite thesis |title=Ecological and socio-economic effects of industrial oil palm plantations in Southwest Cameroon |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.53846/goediss-7974 |publisher=University Goettingen Repository |first=Denis |last=Kupsch}}</ref> and forages from the canopy of trees.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Auerbach |first=Michael |last2=Shmida |first2=Avi |date=March 1993 |title=Vegetation Change Along an Altitudinal Gradient on Mt Hermon, Israel--No Evidence for Discrete Communities |journal=The Journal of Ecology |volume=81 |issue=1 |page=25 |doi=10.2307/2261221 |issn=0022-0477}}</ref>

Olive long-tailed cuckoos do not flock in groups.<ref name=":0" />
